$comment: Wave gates. Each gate names a deterministic command list the manager runs before merging staging → trunk (Phase 6/7). See _template-gate.json for the field shape and orchestration/prompts/manager-kickoff.md §8 for the gate-check semantics. Active gates (the wave currently in flight) live in `gates`; planned gates live in `future_gates` and get promoted to `gates` at the start of each wave's Phase 4. The G.design-sync entry below ships with the template — it is a no-op when clients/designs/ has no surfaces, and becomes blocking once the first surface is added.

gates.description: Verify every clients/designs/<surface>/design.yaml is parseable, has matching snapshots on disk, and that maps_to_req cross-refs resolve. No-op when no surfaces exist.

Wave 1 close-out gate: catalog + track-meta + typecheck + full test suite + design-sync must all pass on the staging branch before merge to trunk. Eligible once all six Wave-1 tracks report [complete:<id>].
